photo_observations.alterations_visible: Modern purpose-built school facility, not a heritage conversion
photo_observations.notes: Modern community school building. Two photos captured from slightly different angles. The main entrance features a covered portico/drive-through area with brick columns.

facade_description: Kensington Community School, ~242 Augusta Ave presents a house-form facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a multiple gables with blue metal cladding, consistent with its house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a brown brick lower level with blue corrugated metal cladding on upper storey and roof palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 6, 4 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
